mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling from restrictive levels, discount rates ease and equity valuations re-rate upward. This predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month, especially when CPIAUCSL is not accelerating.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines at least 25 bps over the prior 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the effect disappears when CPIAUCSL inflation is flat to rising.

mechanism_narrative: An upward break in UNRATE signals labor market deterioration that compresses expected earnings and raises recession odds, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are positive in most trigger events or if UNRATE increases without concurrent PAYEMS weakness and the signal still predicts losses.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly drop in RSAFS indicates weakening consumer spending and revenue growth, which lowers near-term earnings expectations and pushes S&P 500 returns lower over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS falls more than 1.0% month over month
failure_condition: Reject if negative RSAFS shocks are not followed by below-median 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect vanishes once GDP growth is stable.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO together with stable employment indicates stronger real output without an immediate labor cost shock, supporting cyclical earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods of sustained INDPRO improvement do not precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if gains occur only when PAYEMS is also accelerating sharply.

mechanism_narrative: A rate-cutting response after inflation has peaked supports equities by lowering real funding costs and signaling policy insurance. The effect is strongest when CPIAUCSL growth is falling and FEDFUNDS is still above neutral-like levels.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation over 3 months is declining
failure_condition: Reject if rate cuts during declining inflation do not generate above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign turns negative when inflation is falling.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE indicates broadening labor demand and stronger household income, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform either proxy alone, or if positive SPX 20-day returns are not concentrated in months with both PAYEMS up and UNRATE down.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts collapse, household formation and construction-related demand weaken, reducing cyclical growth expectations and pushing S&P 500 lower over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST declines are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect only appears when other demand series are also weakening.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in RSAFS and HOUST signals broad final-demand strength across consumption and housing, which should improve earnings expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint rise in sales and housing does not outperform single-series signals, or if triggered periods fail to deliver positive 20-day SPX returns more often than chance.
